archimate:BusinessService.name: Data Space
archimate:BusinessService.documentation:
Definition: The term Data Space refers to a type of data relationship between trusted partners who adhere to the same high level standards and guidelines in relation to data storage and sharing within one or many Vertical Ecosystems.
Source: Gaia-X
Source reference: https://gaia-x.eu/what-is-gaia-x/deliverables/data-spaces/
Additional information: The term ‘data space’ refers to a type of data relationship between trusted partners who adhere to the same high level standards and guidelines in relation to data storage and sharing within one or many Vertical Ecosystems.
Example: Several examples of applying data spaces in different sectors:
LOST view: TVA-Data Space Enablers [Motivation]
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DataSpaceBusinessService
ABB name: egovera:DataSpaceBusinessService
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DataSpaceBusinessService
Name: Data Space Digital Public Service
Interoperability Layer: Organisational
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/DigitalPublicBusinessService

properties.value: Several examples of applying data spaces in different sectors:

The term ‘data space’ refers to a type of data relationship between trusted partners who adhere to the same high level standards and guidelines in relation to data storage and sharing within one or many Vertical Ecosystems.

The term Data Space refers to a type of data relationship between trusted partners who adhere to the same high level standards and guidelines in relation to data storage and sharing within one or many Vertical Ecosystems.
